verification_gate: A red test reproduces the PR 61 and PR 62 CI failure while the same source passes locally; generation in distinct timezone, path, clock, and commit-time fixtures is byte-identical; write followed by check passes; a real source change still produces detectable drift; CI and local verification share one contract.

gate_result.red_first: red test committed at lifeos e8a8788 reproducing the exact cascade: the committed runtime report drifted with the invocation shape (bun run vs direct execution) and carried the machine-specific /nix/store executable path plus absolute argv entries, so navigation digests differed across environments — local passes, every other machine stale; green at 7fe6eb7
gate_result.root_cause: verification_runtime_report.json recorded the physical invocation (command string, npm_lifecycle_event, process.execPath, absolute argv). The nav index digests this state file, so any environment where the bun store path or invocation shape differed from the writer's saw stale navigation — the PR 61/62 signature
gate_result.contract_evidence: the report now records the canonical entrypoint constant, basename executable, canonical lifecycle event, and basename-reduced out-of-repo argv; no machine-specific absolute path enters the committed byte surface
gate_result.fixture_evidence: the regression test runs the real verifier via lifecycle-shaped and direct invocation, under TZ=Asia/Tokyo, and repeatedly — all four committed reports byte-identical (clock is already pinned by SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH; path portability is asserted by rejecting absolute argv entries)
gate_result.see_saw_evidence: running the verifier (either invocation shape) after a navigation write leaves build-navigation-index --check green — the verify↔navigation see-saw that forced nav rewrites after every verify is gone
gate_result.drift_evidence: adopting the normalized contract itself changed the report bytes exactly once and correctly re-staled navigation until rewritten — genuine source changes remain detectable
gate_result.full_suite: navigation spec 10/10 (including the new byte-stability regression), proof-ledger/execution-contracts/navigation-counts 20/20, python suites OK
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: the regression test spawns the real verifier binary four times against the real repository and compares the committed bytes

proof_summary: ARCHBP-037 is complete: navigation generation is byte-deterministic across local and CI environments because the one non-deterministic input — the verifier's committed runtime report — now encodes a single deterministic environment contract. The red test (lifeos e8a8788) reproduced the PR 61/62 signature exactly: invocation-shape drift plus the machine-specific /nix/store executable path in the committed report meant navigation digests could never match across machines. The green fix (7fe6eb7) normalizes command, lifecycle event, executable, and argv to logical identities; the regression proves byte-identical reports across invocation shapes, timezones, and repeated runs while genuine source changes still produce detectable drift. As a direct consequence the verify↔navigation see-saw is eliminated: write-then-check passes and stays green through subsequent verifier runs.
